word: G5352 - φθινοπωρινός - phthinopōrinós - fthin-op-o-ree-nos' - Whose fruit withereth

sections.paragraph:
- Original: φθινοπωρινός

- Transliteration: Phthinoporinos

- Phonetic: fthin-op-o-ree-nos'

- Definition:

1. autumn trees

a. trees such as they are at the close of autumn, dry, leafless and without fruit

b. metaph. of unfruitful, worthless men

- Origin: from derivative of phthino (to wane

- TDNT entry: akin to the base of 5351) and 3703 (meaning late autumn)

- Part(s) of speech:

- Strong's: From a derivative of φθίνω phthinō (to wane; akin to the base of G5351) and G3703 (meaning late autumn) autumnal (as stripped of leaves): - whose fruit withereth.
